title: Jon Sumrall is the bluntest talent evaluating coach ever
description: Want a blunt evaluation of your NFL Draft stock? Jon Sumrall seems like the type that would give it to you.
fullText:
First year Florida head coach Jon Sumrall does not sugarcoat it. If you want an accurate assessment of where you stand as a player, how good or bad you are, Sumrall could likely tell you. Want a blunt evaluation of your NFL Draft stock? Sumrall seems like the type that would give it to you.

It's no secret that Florida will be building their offense around talented tailback Jadan Baugh, one of the top running back prospects in in the class of 2027, this season. The Gators are breaking in a new QB1, and according to Sumrall, he won't have to exactly set the college football world on fire with his play, this season.

"None of them need to be Tim Tebow," Sumrall said at SEC Media Days, alluding to the man who won a Heisman Trophy and two national championships in Gainesville.

"They need to be somebody that just manages a game and operates the right way at the line scrimmage and doesn't turn the football over. We've got decent skill players around the quarterback position with Jadan Baugh and the receiver group."

In saying "game manager" and "hey, just don't turn the ball over," Sumrall is basically saying "you don't have to be good, but just don't be awful."

Georgia Tech transfer Aaron Philo is said to have the slight upper hand in the QB competition right now. However, he faces a stiff training camp position battle against second year Gators QB Tramell Jones Jr.

Sumrall expounded more on the quarterback situation:

"Do we have a Heisman Trophy candidate? No, but they both have enough arm talent. They both are adequate runners. Neither one of them are elite runners. Neither one of them are slugs either. Both have good arms, both have good releases. Both can make all the throws. So there's enough there for us to win with."

Again he makes the QB room sound pretty mid right now.

Sumrall is going viral right now for his faking outrage over a reporter's cellphone going off in the middle of his media session.

"You'd be kicked out of our team meeting ... You're lucky I'm not gonna throw this freakin' podium." 😅A phone went off during Jon Sumrall's press conference 😬 pic.twitter.com/0Xsy7sball

— SEC Network (@SECNetwork) July 22, 2026

However, that sequence may have actually been the least interesting thing that he said on Wednesday.

This quote, about his upbringing in Alabama, has the internet buzzing: “I grew up thinking there were three leagues: the AFC, the NFC and the SEC."

While it's meant as a compliment to the league where "it just means more," it also just serves a joke right up on a silver platter. Or at least it does for those who might be cynical about how the SEC may have operated in the pre-NIL era.

This article originally appeared on Draft Wire: Jon Sumrall is the bluntest talent evaluating coach ever

title: Jake Paul claims NFL teams have reached out to sign him to address ‘super mediocrity’ among players
description: Jake Paul might be eyeing a major career transition, as he pivots from the boxing ring to the NFL gridiron. Paul is perhaps the most polarizing figure in all of sports today.
fullText:
Jake Paul might be eyeing a major career transition, as he pivots from the boxing ring to the NFL gridiron.

Paul is perhaps the most polarizing figure in all of sports today. And he has managed to translate his notorious reputation into a lucrative career as one of the biggest draws in combat sports today.

But after a crushing defeat to Anthony Joshua in December, could Paul be leaving behind his gloves for a helmet and some shoulder pads?

Speaking to Front Office Sports, Jake Paul revealed that he has had conversations with multiple NFL teams and coaches about possibly transitioning to the NFL.

Paul then took a dig at a large number of NFL players, suggesting that he is a better athlete than them, and that he would pursue an NFL career once he wraps up his boxing career.

“It’s pretty simple. Just like when I started boxing, it’s funny to see the Tweet-and-deleters out there, the non-believers, the non-sayers, shoutout to Drake.

“I think there is a lot of super-mediocrity in the NFL, a lot of not-good athletes, a lot of people dropping passes on a consistent basis.

“I have better hands than them, I’m faster than them, I’m stronger than them, I work harder than them. Really, all it is going to take is just me after boxing, practicing some routes.

“I have a couple of teams and coaches that I have talked to that are interested in bringing me on to the team. It’s really going to be quite easy.”

When asked which team in the NFL he would want to play for, Paul claimed that he wanted to represent his hometown team, the Cleveland Browns.

“I’d love to go to the Cleveland Browns, I’ve been a Browns fan my whole entire life. My mom is a die-hard Browns fan and I think they could use some good receivers over there. Reserve the #17 Browns jersey right now, thank you.”

Paul grew up in Cleveland, so his desire to play for the Browns makes sense, especially considering their lack of success over the last two decades.

It appears Paul is trying to transition from combat sports to football, something Brock Lesnar attempted in 2004 when he left the WWE.

Lesnar managed to make it to the Minnesota Vikings practice squad before being cut, but the University of Minnesota standout had some prior high school football experience to justify the endeavor.

Paul does not have the same football experience, nor the explosive athleticism that Lesnar did, so he might find this transition a lot tougher.

title: The other ESPN analyst set to profit from recent firings as well as Pat McAfee
description: Ryan Clark and a number of his ESPN colleagues were fired earlier this week, but there are some analysts who could be set to profit from the recent cutbacks. The developments over at ESPN ahead of the 2026 NFL season have caused shockwaves across the league.
fullText:
Ryan Clark and a number of his ESPN colleagues were fired earlier this week, but there are some analysts who could be set to profit from the recent cutbacks.

The developments over at ESPN ahead of the 2026 NFL season have caused shockwaves across the league.

Some major exits have been seen from the broadcasting company, with the likes of NFL insider Tom Pelissero also losing his job.

However, ESPN are doubling down on some of their top analysts, with new deals expected to be handed out over the coming weeks.

There is an understanding that Pat McAfee could receive a significant pay rise as ESPN have now created some headroom in their finances.

McAfee may not be the only one set to profit, with ESPN also handing out a new deal to Tim Tebow.

The former 25 overall pick from the 2010 draft truly made a name for himself in college football after winning the Heisman trophy.

Tebow has covered college football for ESPN since 2014, making him one of the household names in their coverage.

“I love this game and getting to talk about it every Saturday with the crews on SEC Network and ESPN is a gift I don’t take lightly,” Tebow said.

“SEC fans bring in a passion that never gets old, and I’m grateful I get to keep doing this alongside people who feel like family.”

NFL fans were stunned to see Pelissero fired earlier this week, in what felt like a major loss for ESPN through their own doing.

There have been a lot of departures over the course of the week, with Clark’s firing being the real standout of them all.

Clark has broken his silence on his ESPN exit, having allowed the dust to settle since his firing on Monday

However, in the midst of numerous people losing their jobs, ESPN have not shied away from handing out new contracts to some of their top talents.

title: LA Rams’ Matthew Stafford Falls to NFL MVP for Best QB in Latest Rankings
description: The Los Angeles Rams spent the offseason feeling like Matthew Stafford is one of the NFL’s otherworldly quarterbacks for at least one more season.A new national ranking offered an endorsement that bolstered that belief.FOX Sports’ Henry McKenna ranked all 32 projected starting quarterbacks heading into training camp and placed Stafford at No. 2.Only Buffalo Bills […] The post LA Rams’ Matthew Stafford Falls to NFL MVP for Best QB in Latest Rankings appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
The Los Angeles Rams spent the offseason feeling like Matthew Stafford is one of the NFL’s otherworldly quarterbacks for at least one more season.

A new national ranking offered an endorsement that bolstered that belief.

FOX Sports’ Henry McKenna ranked all 32 projected starting quarterbacks heading into training camp and placed Stafford at No. 2.

Only Buffalo Bills quarterback Josh Allen finished above him.

Stafford landed ahead of Joe Burrow, Jordan Love, Justin Herbert, Drake Maye and Patrick Mahomes.

McKenna described the exercise as an early look toward Week 1, with quarterback readiness, supporting casts and track records among the considerations.

The placement gives the Rams another reason to enter training camp with Super Bowl expectations.

Stafford earned the ranking after producing the best statistical season of his 17-year career.

The 38-year-old led the NFL with 4,707 passing yards and 46 touchdown passes while throwing eight interceptions. He also finished second with a 109.2 passer rating, led the league with 236 passing first downs and helped Los Angeles finish 12-5.

His efficiency became historic during the middle of the season.

Stafford became the first quarterback in NFL history to throw at least four touchdown passes without an interception in three consecutive regular-season games. He later set a league record by throwing 28 consecutive touchdown passes without an interception.

Those numbers earned Stafford the first Associated Press NFL MVP of his career and a first Associated Press NFL MVP. The Rams then signed him to a contract extension in May, removing one potential distraction before his 18th season.

The ranking still stopped one place short of putting Stafford at the top.

Allen passed for 3,668 yards with 25 touchdowns and 10 interceptions last season, although his rushing ability continues to give Buffalo another dimension.

FOX Sports nevertheless placed Stafford above several younger quarterbacks carrying their own championship expectations.

That group includes Mahomes, who fell to No. 7 as he works back from a torn ACL, and Burrow, who ranked third after an injury-shortened season.

For Stafford, No. 2 represents another sign that age has done little to weaken his standing around the league.

The regular-season production only tells part of the story.

Stafford completed 22 of 35 passes for 374 yards and three touchdowns in the NFC Championship Game. The Rams still fell 31-27 to the Seattle Seahawks after defensive and special teams mistakes left the offense chasing the game.

According to the Rams, Stafford became the first quarterback to throw for at least 350 yards and three touchdowns without a turnover and lose a playoff start. Puka Nacua caught nine passes for 165 yards and a touchdown, but Los Angeles could not complete its final comeback.

That ending reinforced how close the Rams are to another Super Bowl appearance.

Los Angeles selected Ty Simpson with the No. 13 pick in the 2026 NFL draft as Stafford’s eventual successor. The Rams have not established a timeline for that transition, and Stafford said during organized team activities that his body felt good as he prepared for another season.

Training camp begins July 27 at Loyola Marymount University. The Rams can manage Stafford’s workload while developing the depth behind him, but there is little uncertainty about which quarterback determines the team’s ceiling.

FOX Sports placed Stafford behind one quarterback in the league.

After an MVP season and a narrow NFC Championship Game loss, the Rams enter camp with expectations to match that ranking.

Stafford has already shown he can play at the required level. The next challenge is turning it into one more Super Bowl run.

title: ‘This Is Huge’ – NFL Podcaster Alleges New Evidence in Matt Miller Fantasy League Scandal
description: ESPN’s NFL Draft analyst Matt Miller has been making headlines recently, but it has had nothing to do with football. Instead, it’s because he’s being investigated by the Missouri attorney general for allegedly scamming his followers out of money he promised would go toward charity.
fullText:
ESPN’s NFL Draft analyst Matt Miller has been making headlines recently, but it has had nothing to do with football.

Instead, it’s because he’s being investigated by the Missouri attorney general for allegedly scamming his followers out of money he promised would go toward charity. This comes after a devastating car accident that required multiple surgeries and a life-saving amputation of his arm.

Now, one NFL podcaster provided some new information on Miller’s alleged scam, resurfacing audio of Miller’s brother potentially incriminating himself.

Earlier this month, the Missouri Attorney General’s Office opened an investigation into Miller. This was in response to numerous people coming forward and alleging that the ESPN analyst was scamming his followers using charity fantasy football leagues.

Miller allegedly started many leagues with an entry fee of $100, with half of the pot supposedly going to charity. However, many of the league’s winners never received their winnings, and there was no proof that the other half of the funds had been sent to charity. Miller was also accused of ghosting individuals who paid him for scouting lessons, but the current investigation focuses on the fantasy football leagues.

According to Awful Announcing, Miller was the commissioner of more than 91 leagues on Sleeper in 2025 alone before he was ultimately banned from the app.

After Miller was involved in a car accident with a semi-truck earlier this year, his family set up a GoFundMe for his followers to help with his medical bills. More than $50,000 was raised and prominent ESPN employees donated, including Pat McAfee, Adam Schefter, and Mina Kimes. However, the GoFundMe brought attention to a Reddit thread with the allegations against Miller, which then went viral on X as more accusations poured in.

REDRAFT THE ENTIRE NFL: PFN’s FREE NFL Ultimate Redraft Simulator

Now, NFL podcaster Tony Farmer believes he’s uncovered new evidence that links Miller’s brother, Marshal, to the scheme.

“Holy crap, I found it. Matt Miller’s brother Marshal acknowledging on tape he goes by ‘EmmandEmm.’ This is huge,” Farmer wrote on X. “Why it matters: 1) Now 2 different nicknames he goes by (also “melloesquire”) – often registered in the SAME Matt Miller charity fantasy leagues. Unless someone else was controlling these accounts with his alias, it appears Marshal Miller secretly controlled TWO teams in some of Miller’s leagues.

“Miller played in each league too so it appears the brothers sometimes controlled 3 of the 12 teams in the league! (There could be teams we don’t know too).”

Farmer further stated that Marshal Miller’s username “EmmandEmm” registered for 19 of Matt Miller’s fantasy leagues between 2024 and 2025. His other username, “MelloEsquire,” registered for 52 of Matt Miller’s fantasy leagues between 2024 and 2025, and both usernames were in the same league 12 times.

BE THE GM OF YOUR FAVORITE TEAM: PFN’s FREE NFL Mock Draft Simulator

“Remember that each owner was expected to pay $100 and half that money was promised to Matt for charity,” Farmer continued. “Do you think Marshal was paying registration fees? If the answer is no, then it raises questions about the actual amount of money that went to charity vs. what was promised.”

It’s unclear if this is accurate, but it’s just the latest aspect of this scandal that leads to more questions than answers. Miller is currently on a leave of absence at ESPN as he recovers from his accident.

Recently, Miller broke his silence on the allegations in an exchange with The Athletic.

“Because this is an active investigation, I’ve been told by legal counsel to limit my response,” Miller said in an email. “However, I’ve tried reaching out to all the winners from the last season to make sure they’ve been paid.

“My [direct messages] on all social media remain open for winners to contact me. Because of my limited access to my original email account, I have had trouble authenticating winners, but everyone who finished in first, second, or third place will be paid out, and I’ve worked hard to make that happen.”

title: 2027 NFL Draft Summer Scouting: Kenyatta Jackson Jr., Edge, Ohio State
description: The Ohio State defense is among the best in the country, but what impact does Kenyatta Jackson Jr. have?
fullText:
Summer is here, and it is everyone’s favorite time of the year, scouting time. Every year, we do Summer Scouting to preview college football for Vikings fans, but also give them an idea of names to follow and watch in the fall as needs pop up for Minnesota.

Are we going to write about quarterbacks? Offensive tackles? Edge rushers? You know it, but why? Because other NFL teams will draft them, just as they will draft every other position. It all matters whether these players end up on the Vikings, the Bears, or the Steelers. We write these so Vikings fans can be the most informed fans.

So if you are still with us, enjoy our latest Summer Scouting report as we prepare you for the Fall.

4-Star prospect according to 247 Sports

Named the Gatorade Player of the Year in the state of Florida

Under Armour All-American in high school

Lines up the most at REO (37% of snaps)

2 Passes batted at the line of scrimmage

One of the better edges in run support

Great tackling technique, wraps up

Ideal size and length to make an impact

Can win with his hand in the dirt

Tremendous power to knock blockers back

Kenyatta Jackson Jr. is a more throwback type of player at the edge rusher position. He strikes me as someone who can win off the edge in a 3-4 scheme and also provide excellent run support. He may not ever be a big sack producer, but he can start, be in packages at the goal line, and provide pressure time and again. He is a safe evaluation already, in my opinion, but he does have some potential to take another step in 2026.

title: 2027 NFL Draft Summer Scouting Report: Kameryn Crawford, Edge, USC
description: The USC defense is making a turn up front and Kameryn Crawford is a big reason why.
fullText:
Summer is here, and it is everyone’s favorite time of the year, scouting time. Every year, we do Summer Scouting to preview college football for Bucs fans, but also give them an idea of names to follow and watch in the fall as needs pop up for Tampa Bay.

Are we going to write about quarterbacks? Offensive tackles? Edge rushers? You know it, but why? Because other NFL teams will draft them, just as they will draft every other position. It all matters whether these players end up on the Bucs, the Bears, or the Steelers. We write these so Bucs fans can be the most informed fans.

So if you are still with us, enjoy our latest Summer Scouting report as we prepare you for the Fall.

4-Star prospect according to 247 Sports

Under Armour All-American in high school

Lines up the most at ROLB (40% of snaps)

0 Passes batted at the line of scrimmage

Quickness and burst off the edge

Good length to get and win leverage

Shows some finesse in his pass rushing

Can generate power with a build-up

Crawford has some physical traits that are being relied upon in their raw form rather than being used in ways that could have led to more success. He is best suited to stand up on the edge rather than have his hand in the dirt to showcase his length and speed off the edge. Crawford has good awareness to provide run support as well, but I struggle to see him do that consistently. The quickness and burst make you want to be in on him as a prospect, but right now, it is hard to be all-in.

title: Carson Beck first among top 4 QBs drafted to sign rookie deal
description: The top four quarterbacks selected in the 2026 NFL Draft were all unsigned as of Wednesday morning. Carson Beck officially ended the gridlock.
fullText:
The top four quarterbacks selected in the 2026 NFL Draft were all unsigned as of morning of Wednesday, July 22. Carson Beck ended the gridlock.

Beck signed his rookie deal, the Arizona Cardinals announced Wednesday. All seven members of the Cardinals’ 2026 draft class, who reported to training camp Wednesday, have now inked their rookie deals.

Beck was drafted in the third round (pick No. 65) in this year’s draft. The 24-year-old rookie is expected to battle with Jacoby Brissett and Gardner Minshew for Arizona’s starting quarterback job.

Beck played 55 college games with Miami (2025) and Georgia (2020-24). He passed for 11,725 yards and 88 touchdowns while compiling a 37-6 record as a starter.

According to Over The Cap, Beck’s four-year contract is worth $7.4 million.

Rookie contracts are slotted and predetermined. But the top four quarterbacks selected in the 2026 draft, Fernando Mendoza, Ty Simpson, Carson Beck and Drew Allar, were all unsigned as of Wednesday morning because of unresolved terms in their contracts.

Quarterbacks typically to have more leverage to negotiate certain details in their rookie contracts. Signing bonus cash flow, guaranteed money details and offset language are negotiable terms in rookie contracts.

Beck, Simpson and Allar are represented by agents part of the Creative Artists Agency (CAA) while Mendoza is represented by an agent in Excel Sports Management.

Now that Beck has signed his rookie deal, expect it to have a domino effect on the rest of the QB rookie class.

Follow USA TODAY Sports' Tyler Dragon on X @TheTylerDragon.

This article originally appeared on USA TODAY Sports: Carson Beck first among top 4 QBs drafted to sign rookie deal

title: FIFA World Cup breaks 32-year Fox and Telemundo record
description: Soccer is perhaps the most popular sport in the world, even if it is not the biggest in the United States of America. The NFL, NBA, etc, are generally more popular than ‘football’, but the 2026 World Cup meant a lot more eyes were on soccer across America this summer.
fullText:
Soccer is perhaps the most popular sport in the world, even if it is not the biggest in the United States of America.

The NFL, NBA, etc, are generally more popular than ‘football’, but the 2026 World Cup meant a lot more eyes were on soccer across America this summer.

In turn, it is perhaps not surprising to see the tournament breaking viewership records on Fox and Telemundo.

The USMNT were knocked out of the 2026 World Cup in the Round of 16 after losing 4-1 to Belgium.

Despite the early exit, it seems like many were still heavily invested in the competition all the way up to the final.

This was made clear after Fox’s Michael Mulvihill tweeted that the final between Spain and Argentina set viewership records on the network.

He stated: “The World Cup Final averaged 66.4 million viewers in the U.S. on FOX and Telemundo combined. 42.5 million on FOX. 23.9 million on Telemundo.

“Other than Super Bowls, this is the largest sports audience since the 1994 Winter Olympics.”

Soccer in America still has some way to go if it is to reach the heights of the Super Bowl, however.

The 2026 showpiece is reported to have had over 125 million viewers on Fox earlier this year, around double that of the World Cup.

Of course, that may have been different had the USMNT reached the final, but that was always going to be an unlikely scenario.

Still, it may lead to more people inside the USA starting to take soccer a bit more seriously, given there is clearly a decent audience to capitalise on.

title: 3 Broncos made AP's top NFL players list (plus Sean Payton as a coach)
description: Three Broncos players (and coach Sean Payton) were named among the best at their respective positions by the Associated Press.
fullText:
With training camp right around the corner, it's officially List Season in the NFL.

The Associated Press recently ranked the top five players at each position group across the league, and three Denver Broncos players made the cut.

Notably, Pat Surtain was named top-ranked cornerback for the second consecutive year.

"Surtain received seven first-place votes from a panel of eight AP pro football writers who ranked the top five players at the position, basing selections on current status entering the 2026 season," Rob Maaddi wrote for AP.

Additionally, Quinn Meinerz was the third-ranked interior offensive lineman and Garett Bolles was the third-ranked offensive tackle. On defense, Zach Allen fell in the also received votes category at defensive tackle.

The squad is led by Sean Payton, who ranked fifth among 32 head coaches in the AP survey.

Surtain, Meinerz and Bolles all received AP All-Pro recognition following the 2025 season, so it's no surprise that they have been ranked among the best players at their respective positions going into the 2026 campaign.

title: Rams fall to dead last in new ESPN ranking
description: The Rams roster is one of the oldest in the NFL but also doesn't have too much talent under the age of 25.
fullText:
The Los Angeles Rams may have one of the best rosters on paper, but it's mainly constructed of veterans rather than long-term developmental players. As such, the Rams' roster ranks 26th-oldest heading into the 2026 season.

This isn't a bad thing, especially for a team with immediate Super Bowl aspirations spearheaded by a 38-year-old quarterback, but it means they checked in at No. 32 in ESPN's latest ranking of NFL teams' under-25 talent.

ESPN built the rankings using starts and snaps logged by players under 25, age-adjusted quality of play with extra weight for Pro Bowl and All-Pro selections, contract value and length, positional importance, draft capital added in the first three rounds of the 2026 draft, projected 2026 depth charts, and any injuries or suspensions likely to affect availability.

Unsurprisingly, the Rams don't have many of those types of players on their roster at the moment.

Perhaps you have heard that the Rams are not as fond of first-round draft picks as other teams? Certainly, trades they have made for veterans contribute to the fact that while Los Angeles has a ton of talent, very little of it is under 25 years old.

The Rams only had two players currently under 25 who started games last season: safety Kamren Kinchens, who will be 24 at the end of September, and tight end Terrance Ferguson, who is also 23. Another one of the tight ends, second-round rookie Max Klare, just turned 23 on July 8. First-round quarterback Simpson will turn 24 in December. The Rams hope Simpson doesn't get on the field for a couple of seasons because they have someone much older they would rather see under center: reigning MVP Matthew Stafford, who is 38 years old.

First-round quarterback Ty Simpson is the lone player ESPN tabs as a blue chip for the Rams' under-25 core, and even he isn't expected to see the field anytime soon behind Stafford. Compounding the shortage, Los Angeles just graduated several of its best young pieces out of eligibility: cornerback Trent McDuffie, offensive tackle Warren McClendon Jr. and wide receiver Puka Nacua all turned 25 and no longer count toward the rankings.

That combination — an aging roster built through trades and free agency, a limited draft footprint and a wave of players aging out right as they hit their prime — leaves the Rams with the league's thinnest projected young talent pool heading into 2026, according to ESPN.

But again, that won't matter if they bring home a Lombardi Trophy.

This article originally appeared on Rams Wire: Rams fall to dead last in new ESPN ranking

title: Tuli Tuipulotu makes NFL's top 100 players list due to elite season
description: Tuli Tuipulotu has become a household name in the NFL as a pass rusher with the Chargers. Can Tuli help the Bolts take the next step?
fullText:
Each season, Tuli Tuipulotu is making his name known as a top pass rusher in the NFL. The Chargers' faith in him is growing. They moved on from former top draft pick Joey Bosa and let Odafe Oweh walk. Tuli Tuipulotu is becoming the cornerstone of the Chargers' defense, and he is getting more respect as a result.

In a recent article, Bobby Kownack breaks down Tuli Tuipulotu making the NFL top 100 players list. Here is what he had to say about Tuipulotu:

"Incremental improvements through his first three years led to a certified breakout for Tuipulotu last season. The 23-year-old officially emerged from the shadow of his pass-rushing mentor Khalil Mack and lapped the rest of L.A’s edge defenders, as well, leading the team in sacks for a second straight campaign to make his first Pro Bowl. His 13 sacks doubled his career total, and his pressure percentage jumped from 10.4 across 2023-24 to 15.6. Now, the question is whether he has yet another leap in store for 2026."

Tuli Tuipulotu continues to develop his game, and this past season, he put it all together, leading to a Pro Bowl nod for him. When teams face the Chargers, #45 is the main guy they are preparing for all week long. Getting on the NFL's top 100 players list is just the beginning for Tuli. This upcoming season should bring even more dominance for the former Trojan.

title: Bengals lose one popular trade target idea before training camp
description: A contract extension elsewhere in the NFL means the Bengals won't be making this suggested trade.
fullText:
Every now and then, the Cincinnati Bengals popped up in Jordyn Brooks trade ideas.

The Dolphins hit Brooks with a contract extension this week, ending any speculation that the new regime in Miami would go ahead and ship away the star linebacker, continuing the major roster blow-up over there.

Brooks was a fun idea, at least. He racked up 183 total tackles last season and is still just 28 years old after being a first-round pick in 2020.

It was easy to link the Bengals and Brooks. The front office in Cincinnati has been unusually aggressive this offseason. Linebacker was the worst spot on a terrible defense last year. And of all the moves the Bengals have made this offseason, they have yet to address linebacker.

Alas, the Bengals are all-in on Demetrius Knight and Barrett Carter. That won’t stop them from making another free-agent signing or trade during training camp in the name of better depth, but they believe the second-year duo remains the way of the future at the needy spot.

This article originally appeared on Bengals Wire: Bengals lose one popular trade target idea before training camp

title: What Stephen A. Smith told the ESPN ‘bosses’ after learning of Ryan Clark’s firing
description: Ryan Clark’s departure from ESPN on Monday has seen numerous big players in the sports media industry share their thoughts on the development. It is believed ESPN have made a number of cost-cutting firings ahead of the new 2026 NFL season getting underway in just a matter of weeks.
fullText:
Ryan Clark’s departure from ESPN on Monday has seen numerous big players in the sports media industry share their thoughts on the development.

It is believed ESPN have made a number of cost-cutting firings ahead of the new 2026 NFL season getting underway in just a matter of weeks.

Clark has been a regular on ESPN having joined the network all the way back in 2015 following a successful career as a safety in the NFL.

However, the nature of Clark’s departure is what has really frustrated some of his colleagues as he was informed of the decision midway through an NFL Live show on ESPN.

It has been suggested that Clark’s confrontational manner contributed to his ESPN firing following a heated exchange with Peter Schrager last year.

However, established talking head Stephen A. Smith has now weighed in on the matter to share his frustrations with the ESPN chiefs.

Clark is just one of 10 members of the ESPN broadcasting team that has been laid off since the start of the week as the cost-cutting exercise takes effect.

Smith has established himself as one of the top voices on ESPN, which is why his latest comments on Clark’s firing will hold some value.

“A person I considered to be the best NFL analyst in America, is no longer a part of ESPN. I can’t even tell y’all how much that hurt,” said Smith on his YouTube channel.

“I’m gonna miss him. I’m not happy with this decision. The bosses know I’m not happy. I made this very clear.”

The departures from ESPN are expected to continue over the coming days as their cutbacks provide some shocking layoffs.

It has even been suggested that NFL Insider Tom Pelissero is one of the names who could follow Clark out of the doors at ESPN.

There are some seriously established figures in the American sports broadcasting business who are heading into the summer as free agents.

Someone like Pelissero would unlikely be out of work for too long, but it does go to show the extent to which ESPN are going to in order to cut back on their costs.

title: Las Vegas Raiders owner Mark Davis weighs in on grass fields movement after the World Cup
description: Las Vegas Raiders owner Mark Davis has broken his silence on the demand for NFL teams to use grass fields on the back of this summer’s World Cup. The Allegiant Stadium was not one of the host venues for the United States at the World Cup, but it is one of the NFL stadiums that uses a natural grass playing surface.
fullText:
Las Vegas Raiders owner Mark Davis has broken his silence on the demand for NFL teams to use grass fields on the back of this summer’s World Cup.

The Allegiant Stadium was not one of the host venues for the United States at the World Cup, but it is one of the NFL stadiums that uses a natural grass playing surface.

There is an argument to be made that a natural surface is easier for Vegas to maintain given it is an indoor venue, which does not expose the grass to the elements.

However, Davis is one of the owners who believes playing on a grass field is largely beneficial for his players’ health and wellbeing.

This is not a view that is shared across the league, with numerous NFL players now protesting their thoughts after seeing grass pitches laid down for the World Cup soccer teams.

As the Dallas Cowboys have already ripped up their grass field at AT&T Stadium, Davis has issued a message on the importance of his players playing on a natural surface.

In recent days, Seattle Seahawks fans have urged their new owners to lay down a grass surface at Lumen Field.

It is proving the hottest topic of discussion across the league, as the players make their voices heard over social media.

“There was no way I was going to build the stadium or anything else to play on artificial turf,” said the 71-year-old.

“I just always felt that the safety issue, number one, and I grew up around football and the grass and mud and the smell of those uniforms after the game.”

The Raiders’ stadium cost just shy of $2 billion to build and Davis did not cost cut by going with an artificial playing surface for his players.

Pat McAfee has backed the NFL players’ protest for grass fields, with Davis also backing the notion it should be an investment from the owners towards their players’ safety.

Owners across the league are happy to pay out huge wages to their star players, but there is a clear hesitation from some of them to splash out on installing and maintaining a grass field.

Witnessing the same stadiums across the nation suddenly install grass for the World Cup will have come as a slap in the face for NFL players across the league.

This is why the movement is picking up steam ahead of the 2026 season getting underway.

fullText:

title: Patrick Mahomes’ changing appearance through the years has NFL fans saying the same thing
description: It has all the fans talking about the Chiefs signal caller as training camp approaches.
fullText:
Patrick Mahomes’ changing appearance through the years has NFL fans saying the same thing originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Patrick Mahomes has collected three Super Bowl championships, three Super Bowl MVP awards and two NFL MVP honors during his remarkable run with the Kansas City Chiefs. Yet after seeing how the quarterback’s appearance has changed over the years, NFL fans were left wondering whether Mahomes has aged at all.

Mahomes has endured plenty of wear and tear while developing into one of the greatest quarterbacks in NFL history. His latest challenge is a torn ACL suffered in December, an injury that has made his recovery one of the biggest storylines surrounding Kansas City entering the 2026 season.

Despite everything Mahomes has experienced since becoming the Chiefs’ starting quarterback, a collection of photos posted Tuesday had fans making the same observation.

He really does not look much different.

The NFL shared a series of photos on Instagram showing Mahomes from 2019 through the 2026 offseason. Although there were a few noticeable changes, including different headbands, fewer wristbands, shorter hair and a thicker goatee, Mahomes’ overall appearance remained strikingly similar.

That immediately caught the attention of fans.

“He looks the exact same. Is he AI?” one fan wrote, according to Athlon Sports.

“Looks exactly the same! Love you Patrick!” another fan commented.

“He hasn’t aged, he just gets more handsome,” one reaction read.

Another fan asked a simple question: “Does he age??? He looks the same.”

“Never will age!!!!” another person added.

The side-by-side comparison was especially interesting because of everything that has happened during the period covered by the photos.

Mahomes was only beginning to establish himself as the face of the NFL in 2019. He had already won his first league MVP award, but his championship run in Kansas City was just getting started. Since then, Mahomes has led the Chiefs to three Super Bowl victories and established himself as the defining quarterback of his generation. Kansas City selected him with the No. 10 overall pick in the 2017 NFL Draft, and he has spent his entire career with the franchise.

His resume has changed dramatically. His appearance apparently has not.

MORE: It just got a lot harder for Cowboys to trade George Pickens

While fans had fun with the photos, Mahomes is currently dealing with a far more serious issue. The Chiefs quarterback continues to recover from the torn ACL he suffered in December. Reports have suggested that he could return as early as Week 1, although Kansas City will have to carefully monitor his progress leading into the season.

The injury came during a disappointing year for the Chiefs, who missed the playoffs for the first time since Mahomes became their full-time starting quarterback. Kansas City responded by making several notable offseason moves.

The Chiefs re-signed longtime Mahomes target Travis Kelce and acquired reigning Super Bowl MVP Kenneth Walker III to strengthen the offense. They also signed Justin Fields, giving the team an experienced option at quarterback while Mahomes works his way back. Fields could provide important insurance if Mahomes is not cleared for the season opener. If Mahomes is ready, Fields could still give Kansas City additional flexibility as a backup and situational weapon.

The Chiefs are counting on those additions to help them return to the postseason, but their championship hopes will ultimately depend on Mahomes’ health. The quarterback in the 2026 photo might look almost identical to the player pictured seven years earlier. Getting back to playing like that younger version of himself after a major knee injury will be the much more important challenge.

fullText:

Carolina Panthers wide receiver Xavier Legette should be prepping for a fight.

With second-round draft pick Chris Brazzell II joining other rookies reporting to the 2026 NFL training camp on Tuesday, Legette should be preparing to defend his presumptive position as WR3, behind Tetairoa McMillan and Jalen Coker.

The first-year speedster could be hard to hold back.

Joining the franchise as the No. 32-overall draft pick in 2024, Legette, an ex-South Carolina standout, embraced the regional fanfare of being new general manager Dan Morgan's first pick, which he traded up to execute.

But two inconsistent seasons later, some NFL insiders predict Brazzell's speed and downfield potential will push the Panthers into trading Legette, who has struggled with untimely drops and mental miscues.

Canales continues backing Legette, holding out hope the 6-foot-3, 227-pounder will create a breakout campaign.

"I think it's a make-or-break year for all of us, every year," Canales said June 9. "Xavier has worked that way. From the minute he's gotten here, he's 100 miles an hour. He's putting in the time in the playbook, he's busting his butt on the field, working on little technical skills."

title: ‘Words the Khoslas need to hear’ – Seattle Seahawks issue clear demand to new owners
description: The Seattle Seahawks are in the process of an ownership change, which will see Vinod Khosla and family take over in the Pacific Northwest. It is still early days in the process, with the Khosla family winning the bidding at an NFL record $9.
fullText:
The Seattle Seahawks are in the process of an ownership change, which will see Vinod Khosla and family take over in the Pacific Northwest.

It is still early days in the process, with the Khosla family winning the bidding at an NFL record $9.61 billion.

There is undoubtedly some big shoes to fill for the Khoslas following an incredibly successful tenure from the Allen family, which provided two Super Bowl trophies.

As the ownership change continues to progress, supporters in Seattle are gearing up to defend their Super Bowl crown in the 2026 season.

However, those supporters have already made something crystal clear to the Khoslas ahead of the week one opener in September against the New England Patriots.

It is proving to be one of the hot topics of the NFL following the recent World Cup games being hosted in the United States.

The Dallas Cowboys have already ripped up their grass field at AT&T Stadium on the back of their final World Cup game being held in Arlington.

However, players across the league are protesting online to see their respective owners keep the real surfaces ahead of the new season getting underway.

There is a belief that playing on grass will reduce the number of serious injuries that occur in the league, with Seahawks fans sending the same message to their new owners.

Having already seen Seahawks safety Julian Love protest online, the fans have now sent their thoughts to the new owners.

“Words the Khoslas need to hear,” replied one fan to Love’s post on X.

“Bet it would extend careers by at least 2 years average,” added another.

The Khoslas have broken the NFL record for a franchise sale by purchasing the Seahawks, which shows the level of investment behind them.

“Let the new owners know!!” said one passionate Seahawks fan.

“World champs deserve the best,” said another.

One of the issues regarding the installation of grass turf in the NFL is the cost to maintain it at a high level.

Another fan even directed their thoughts towards the NFL, they said: “@NFL get your players some real grass, don’t just cater to other sports and countries.”

“Are the about-to-be-new #Seahawks owners hearing this?” said another.

How well the grass turf could be maintained in the rainy Pacific Northwest is a big question that may well see the artificial surface return to Lumen Field for the time being.

title: The men behind Trump's $3.47 gas: An NFL coach, a GOP fundraiser and two New Jersey brothers
description: President Donald Trump and his White House have enthusiastically promoted the Freedom Fuel Network, a chain of star-spangled convenience stores selling gas at $3.47 per gallon in honor of the 47th pre...
fullText:
WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump and his White House have enthusiastically promoted the Freedom Fuel Network, a chain of star-spangled convenience stores selling gas at $3.47 per gallon in honor of the 47th president.

Untangling exactly who is behind the Philadelphia-area venture has proven difficult. Records indicate the chain, which was launched last month, is run by a disparate collection of businessmen that includes an NFL kicking coach, a GOP fundraiser and a New Jersey entrepreneur who this year was ordered, along with his brother, to pay civil damages for unlawfully taking more than 200,000 gallons of fuel.

How the stations got Trump's attention remains a mystery, and the four businessmen declined to — or could not be reached for — comment.

What is clear: Trump loved the idea of cheaper gas. The president celebrated the network's gas prices just before the busy July 4th travel period as consumers were grappling with higher oil prices sparked by the war with Iran.

"I am pleased to announce that a VERY smart Retailer, located throughout the Northeast, is stepping up," Trump wrote about the company on his Truth Social platform on July 1. "America has never been stronger than it is now, and Gas Prices will soon be back to the Record Low Prices Americans enjoyed at the pump before our very successful 'excursion' in Iran."

The White House followed up with a post on X a few days later that heralded the opening of the first Freedom Fuel station and produced a video showing patrons waving wads of cash and thanking Trump for reducing the prices outside a store festooned with American flags and a golden eagle logo.

That station, located in Dresher, Pennsylvania, is owned by a subsidiary of Blue Owl Capital, an investment firm, records show. Trump has owned up to $25 million worth of Blue Owl stock, though his most recent financial disclosure says he has sold almost all of that stake.

The White House denied Trump had any personal connection to the venture, but declined to say how the project was developed. It acknowledged having discussions with individuals who set up the network of gas stations.

"The Administration is not involved in the company, nor has the Administration given the company any funding. There is no other entity or person subsidizing the lower gasoline costs," the White House wrote in a statement.

Blue Owl owns about a third of the Freedom Fuel properties, though the company said it leases the stores to independent contractors and "is not involved in the tenant's operations or business decisions."

Fourteen stations in the 25-location network are controlled by companies linked to Shamikh and Syed Kazmi, two brothers who have been dogged by a string of civil misconduct accusations, including fraud, records show.

Shamikh Kazmi is leasing eight of those stations from Blue Owl, according to state records and people familiar with the businesses who spoke on condition of anonymity to discuss the matter. The Associated Press was able to link the brothers to six other Freedom Network locations through records that show they listed those stations' addresses as headquarters for other ventures or supplied those locations with fuel.

A White House official, who insisted on anonymity to discuss the project, said that no one at the White House who was in conversations with Freedom Fuel Network had specifically spoken or worked with Syed Kazmi, a claim that indicates discussions occurred with the other brother, Shamikh.

The Kazmis have marketed themselves as "top tier" petroleum distributors and gas station operators, with over 75 years of experience and a deep well of corporate affiliations, according to an archived version of the website for one of their companies.

Public records offer a more nuanced portrait, showing the Kazmis have been repeatedly sued by companies they had dealings with.

Legal filings detail a series of judgments against the Kazmis, who failed to comply with a court order and have been accused of obscuring their finances and dodging service processors as a growing list of former suppliers and franchisors sought payment.

In February, a federal judge in New Jersey ordered the Kazmis to pay over $600,000 to a fuel supplier that accused the brothers of stealing gas. The supplier alleged in court filings that it cut the brothers off after they refused to sign a new contract. But the Kazmis exploited a security lapse and gained access to the supplier's fuel depot. Over a ten-day period in August 2021, tanker trucks absconded with more than 230,000 gallons of fuel, according to the supplier's court filings. A judge ruled in the supplier's favor, finding the brothers unlawfully took the gas.

The fuel supplier says it has yet to receive payment.

Syed Kazmi was hit with a $380,000 judgment two years ago in a suit brought by 7-Eleven, the convenience chain, which accused him of "dishonest, unethical, immoral" conduct while operating a franchise in Lawrenceville, New Jersey, that was flagged for unsanitary conditions that included trash issues and a rodent infestation. The company also said "tens of thousands of dollars" of cigarettes ordered from 7-Eleven on credit had gone missing.

A federal judge held a company operated by Shamikh Kazmi in contempt in 2022 in a trademark case brought by BP America.

Though BP had severed ties with Kazmi's Diwan Petrol two years prior to the legal action, the corporation's signage had not been removed from the gas station despite a court order to do so. The judge authorized U.S. Marshals to accompany BP workers to remove it.

A man who answered a call to a number listed for the Kazmi brothers said he was not the right person to talk to and instead directed inquiries to the Freedom Fuel Network's website. But that website has no contact information, phone number or mailing address. A request for comment submitted through an online contact form was not answered.

Records from Delaware, a state known for offering incorporators a large degree of opacity, show the Freedom Fuel Network was registered on June 23. The document forming the company was signed by Randy Brown and Yoni Gontownik.

Politico and the website The Newsground reported that Brown is a senior special teams coach with the Baltimore Ravens. He has also served as the elected mayor of Evesham Township, a New Jersey suburb of Philadelphia, where a Freedom Fuel Network station is located.

Brown, a Republican, considered a run for Congress in 2021, telling a local newspaper he was a conservative and a "proud Trump supporter."

Gontownik is a former investment director at Mercuria, a Swiss-owned commodities trading firm. He and his wife live in northern New Jersey and have been active with the pro-Israel political action committee NORPAC, including hosting fundraisers for Republican members of Congress.

Gontownik and Brown did not respond to requests for comment.

Jeff Lenard, a spokesman for the National Association of Convenience Stores, said Freedom Fuel's rock bottom promotional price meant the chain was likely selling the gas at a loss.

"It's not unusual for retailers to have prices that are different than a market when they're looking to make a splash," said Lenard, whose association's members account for most U.S. retail fuel sales, adding that such a splash typically lasts "a matter of hours or a matter of days."

Social media posts and gas-price checking websites show that the per-gallon rate advertised at Freedom Fuel locations began creeping up this week.

A Freedom Fuel station in Bensalem, Pennsylvania, for example, on Thursday was selling regular gasoline for $3.82 a gallon. That was 27 cents cheaper than at a Sunoco station across the street.

The Freedom Fuel Network posted a note of thanks to its website this week, crediting Trump's "strong endorsement" for the "explosive growth" of their business.

"Despite the misinformation and baseless speculation circulating," the statement reads, "let us set the record straight: Freedom Fuel Network is proudly lowering its prices to benefit our community."

title: Ex-Eagles Star Gets Necessary Trade Push From NFL Analyst
description: All NFL offseason long, Josh Sweat’s name has been tossed in trade rumors. The former Philadelphia Eagles defensive end has been described as frustrated with the Arizona Cardinals. When OTAs were starting up across the league, many NFL rumors hinted that Sweat could see a change. The Eagles were often linked as trade candidates for […] The post Ex-Eagles Star Gets Necessary Trade Push From NFL Analyst appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
All NFL offseason long, Josh Sweat’s name has been tossed in trade rumors. The former Philadelphia Eagles defensive end has been described as frustrated with the Arizona Cardinals.

When OTAs were starting up across the league, many NFL rumors hinted that Sweat could see a change. The Eagles were often linked as trade candidates for their former draft pick.

But with training camp quickly approaching, the Sweat rumor mill has gone silent. Bleacher Report’s NFL writer Moe Moton is pushing the Cardinals to kick it back up, by listing a Sweat trade as something that needs to get done before training camp.

GLENDALE, ARIZONA – NOVEMBER 23: Josh Sweat #10 of the Arizona Cardinals looks on from the line in the game against the Jacksonville Jaguars at State Farm Stadium on November 23, 2025 in Glendale, Arizona. (Photo by Norm Hall/Getty Images)

“Josh Sweat needs a change of scenery,” Moton wrote.

“He’s played most of his career with the Philadelphia Eagles, a perennial playoff contender. This offseason, the Arizona Cardinals fired lead skipper Jonathan Gannon, who coached the veteran edge-rusher during their time together in Philadelphia.”

Sweat is reportedly unhappy in Arizona, as Moton noted. He is a win-now player, coming from a competitive NFC team. Sweat was recruited by his ex-Eagles coordinator. Now that Gannon’s out of the picture, the situation seems to be difficult for Sweat to overcome.

GLENDALE, ARIZONA – OCTOBER 05: Josh Sweat #10 of the Arizona Cardinals during the NFL game at State Farm Stadium on October 05, 2025 in Glendale, Arizona. The Titans defeated the Cardinals 22-21. (Photo by Christian Petersen/Getty Images)

In the same rundown of moves that need to happen, Moton suggested the Eagles should pick up the veteran defensive end, Von Miller. It would be easier, as Miller is a free agent and would require just a one-year commitment.

Sweat, on the other hand, is going into year two of a four-year, $76.4 million contract. He’s owed a lot and would need to be acquired via trade. Of course, the Cardinals aren’t going to accept pennies for the 12-sack pass rusher.

PHILADELPHIA, PA – DECEMBER 22: Josh Sweat #94 of the Philadelphia Eagles celebrates his sack on Dak Prescott #4 of the Dallas Cowboys during the fourth quarter at Lincoln Financial Field on December 22, 2019 in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Dallas Cowboys 17-9. (Photo by Corey Perrine/Getty Images)

Making the call is worth it, to at least figure out a price. If it’s within reason, the Eagles shouldn’t hesitate to pounce.

In 17 games with the Cardinals last season, Sweat racked up 30 total tackles, with 13 going for a loss. His 12.0 sacks marked a career high.

The last time the Eagles had Sweat in action, he picked up 41 tackles and 8.0 sacks. During the Super Bowl run, Sweat had 10 tackles, three for a loss, and 2.5 sacks.

title: ‘It’s About Showing Respect’ — Ex-Ravens DB Sends Stern Message To Jaxson Dart Amid Rising Concerns
description: Last year, when rookie Jaxson Dart made his New York Giants debut, he breathed life into a fanbase in desperate need of a pick-me-up. The 25th overall pick in the first round of the 2025 NFL Draft flashed true playmaking ability.
fullText:
Last year, when rookie Jaxson Dart made his New York Giants debut, he breathed life into a fanbase in desperate need of a pick-me-up. The 25th overall pick in the first round of the 2025 NFL Draft flashed true playmaking ability.

Yet, at times, a reckless play style led to turmoil. For Dart, it meant dealing with concussion symptoms that knocked him out of one game and kept him out of the next two. Now one former NFL defensive back has a message for the Giants’ 23-year-old franchise quarterback.

Over the weekend, Giants running back Cam Skattebo drew criticism at the Fanatics Fest for a failed backflip attempt. The frustrations didn’t stem from the fact that he didn’t complete the acrobatic move successfully; rather, it was because he’s pulling risky stunts away from the NFL field.

That sparked a conversation on ESPN’s “Get Up” program, to which former NFLPA president and current ESPN NFL analyst Domonique Foxworth had some advice for Dart. He says the young QB needs to make better “business decisions” out of respect for his teammates.

But that doesn’t mean eliminating Dart’s trademark aggressive nature on the field that fans have come to love him for.

“I’d be much more concerned about it from a quarterback standpoint than from the running back’s standpoint,” Foxworth said.

“You have to get to a point where Dart understands that it’s not only putting yourself at risk. It’s about showing respect to everybody else on this team.”

BUILD YOUR OWN TOP 100: PFN’s FREE NFL Top 100 Builder

So, how does Dart do that? Foxworth suggests Dart would be better off practicing the mantra of living to see another down. Take what the defense gives you, and learn to trust your teammates.

But Foxworth adds that Dart doesn’t have to change who he is as a player; he can still make plays with his feet, he just needs to be smarter.

BE THE GM OF YOUR FAVORITE TEAM: PFN’s FREE NFL Mock Draft Simulator

“You don’t have to win every play,” Foxworth stressed. “You don’t have to win every single time you touch the ball. There are other people who can make plays, and nobody eats if you’re hurt.

“That’s the responsibility part. The aggressiveness, the linebacker nature of his mindset? That’s not the problem. You gotta be able to make a smart business decision. That’s the challenge, and I think he’ll get there.”

Foxworth is a former defensive back who played seven NFL seasons, including with the Baltimore Ravens from 2009 to 2011 until knee problems ended his career. Now, the former Harvard grad is offering his wisdom to Dart, and he believes the Giants QB will figure it out.

After all, Dart was just a rookie last season. He was still getting used to the size, strength, and speed of the NFL.

It usually takes young players a few seasons to get fully adjusted to making the jump from college to the pros, and Dart had his fair share of learning moments last season. Whether he learns from them and shows “respect” to his teammates by making smarter decisions remains to be seen.

title: 49ers have two of the best depth players in the NFL at two positions
description: The San Francisco 49ers have some really talented starters, which also means that some of their backups could play significant roles on other teams.
fullText:
The San Francisco 49ers have a strong roster in 2025, which helped them overcome injuries to several key players and still make the postseason and knock out the defending Super Bowl champions in the wild-card round.

With all of that talent, it means that the 49ers also have some players sitting on the bench this year who are capable of playing important roles for the team if necessary or would possibly start on another team.

In fact, ESPN's Bill Barnwell recently released his All-Backup Team for 2026, and San Francisco had two players on it: linebacker Luke Gifford and safety Marques Sigle.

"The Niners provide two depth pieces on defense," Barnwell wrote. "Gifford isn't someone teams want to have on the field regularly as a defender, but he's a valuable special teams contributor, which is where he'll be useful for our team. Sigle began last season as a starting safety for the 49ers before eventually fading into a backup and special teams role as the year went along. We're taking a shot on him improving in Year 2."

With all of the injuries on defense last year, especially at linebacker, Gifford played 172 defensive snaps, which was the second-most in his seven seasons. He also played well enough in the third phase of the game to make his first Pro Bowl. That's important depth.

Sigle was San Francisco's second fifth-round pick in the 2025 NFL draft, and after starting early in the year with Malik Mustapha recovery from injury, he ended up playing very little after Week 8. There's a chance that Sigle turns things around in Year 2 after a full season in the NFL, but he has to show an improvement in training camp first.

title: Ryan Clark learns of ESPN layoff on live TV
description: Ryan Clark faced a shocking moment on NFL Live when he learned of his ESPN layoff during a live broadcast.
fullText:
Ryan Clark faced a bizarre twist during his time on ESPN. The former safety learned he was fired while on air. This happened during a live appearance on the show NFL Live. The situation created an incredibly awkward scene for everyone watching. Fans saw a professional broadcaster get hit with bad news in real time. It is a nightmare scenario for any media personality. Clark had to keep talking while processing the sudden end of his job. The network did not give him a heads up beforehand. This move stunned the sports world and left many asking questions.

Reports confirm the exact timing of this strange event. Clark was in the middle of a segment when the news broke. He found out he was being let go by the network right then. The NY Post Sports reported the incident as an awkward moment. Clark did not know his contract was ending until that specific second. He continued to speak to his co-hosts despite the shock. The situation unfolded without any warning signs for the audience. Viewers watched a veteran analyst react to the sudden change. It was not a planned segment about career moves. The layoff was immediate and handled poorly by the network. Clark had to navigate the rest of the show while knowing his time was up. The broadcast continued even as the reality set in for him. This method of termination is rare in major sports media. Most companies call employees privately before making any public announcement. ESPN chose to let the news break on live television instead. The lack of preparation showed a disconnect between management and talent. Clark remained professional throughout the entire awkward interaction. He did not lash out or cause a scene on air. The incident highlighted the brutal nature of the sports business. Per reports, the decision came down to budget cuts or strategy shifts. The network did not offer a public explanation for the timing. Clark’s reaction was one of shock mixed with forced composure. He had to finish his segment while his world changed. This event will be talked about for years in the industry.

This incident shows how cold the sports media world can be. Networks often treat talent like disposable assets when money gets tight. Clark deserved a private phone call instead of a public firing. It is disrespectful to an employee to fire them on air. The move hurts ESPN’s reputation among current and future analysts. Trust between talent and management will now be harder to build. Clark handled it with class, but the network failed him. Fans feel bad for seeing a pro treated this way. It sets a terrible precedent for how companies handle layoffs. No one should have to learn they are fired while on camera. This approach creates fear and anxiety for everyone else on staff. It proves that loyalty means very little in big business today. The sports world expects professionalism from everyone involved. ESPN did not show professionalism in this specific instance. The awkwardness will linger for the network and the host. It serves as a warning for other broadcasters about job security.

Clark must now decide his next career move quickly. He has the experience and name recognition to land another job. Other networks will likely reach out to him soon. Fans will want to see where he lands next. The sports community will rally behind him during this transition. He will probably take a break before announcing his future. The incident might actually boost his value as a respected voice. Networks know he handled the pressure with grace. His future looks bright despite this rough start. title: Former Falcons LB makes PFF's 'underappreciated' signings list
description: Kaden Elliss-to-Saints among NFL's most 'underappreciated' free-agent signings
fullText:
The top priority for Matt Ryan in his first offseason leading the Atlanta Falcons' front office seems to have been retaining the team's top players rather than focusing on new acquisitions.

Ryan has done a good job of taking care of the major pieces, re-signing wide receiver Drake London and tight end Kyle Pitts to long-term contract extensions. However, he didn't manage to keep every important piece for the Falcons - and one player that left in free agency could come back to bite them in a bad way.

That would be inside linebacker Kaden Elliss, who had served in the green dot role for Atlanta's defense, which now falls to Divine Deablo. Elliss returned to the New Orleans Saints on a three-year, $33 million deal that the Falcons probably could have matched or beaten if they really wanted to. Now, he'll be suiting up for their most bitter rival and could be a big x-factor in the NFC South race.

According to Pro Football Focus, Elliss re-joining the Saints is one of five underppreciated acquisitions around the NFL from this offseason.

"During his three-year tenure in Atlanta, Elliss performed at a high level but was subject to poor surrounding play, not to mention subpar team results. However, he could be in for a season in which many more become familiar with his game... Elliss’ versatility could add new layers to a unit that punched above its weight class. If Elliss can act as an every-down playmaker in 2026 on a growing defense, it should only help the blossoming Saints make more noise."

Deablo is an equally underrated defender who had an excellent 2025 season across the board, earning strong grades in run defense, pass rushing and coverage.

However, the rest of Atlanta's depth chart at linebacker looks a lot more bare without Elliss in the fold - and if Christian Harris can't fill the void Elliss left behind then the Falcons may be forced to start a rookie at the other linebacker spot - either LSU's Harold Perkins Jr. or Oklahoma's Kendal Daniels.

The 2016 Falcons defense was a rare exception to the rule, but rookies tend not to thrive in their first year in the NFL - especially Day 3 draft picks. Much depends on how well Harris will perform next to Deablo, but Elliss may end up helping the Saints surpass Atlanta in any case.

This article originally appeared on Falcons Wire: Former Falcons LB makes PFF's 'underappreciated' signings list

title: Las Vegas Raiders Summer Scouting: WR Rankings
description: Scouting, Evaluating, and Previewing the 2027 NFL Draft WR Class for the Las Vegas Raiders
fullText:
I broke down the 2027 scouting classes in my early summer scouting series, and the room has a ton of potential with a long list of potential standout players. I broke down the offense and defense in articles and I suggest reading those for a broad aspect of the room.

These rankings are extremely early, and they will evolve over not only the summer but the next 8-10 months throughout the season, and into draft season. Some lists will feature 10, 15, 20, or more ranked prospects but I’ll include others on the list below to note.

Jeremiah Smith, Ohio State – 6’3, 225, Jr

Smith has broken into the notion for 2027 since his freshman season, and he’s not only arguably the top player in the 2027 class but also the best Ohio State WR in the last 5-6 years. Smith has an elite frame, has a career 163 receptions, 2558 yards, and 27 touchdowns. He has an electric release package, explosive release out of cuts, shows an elite ability to go out of his frame, work the boundary, and come down with a ton of high contested catches. Smith’s route running is smooth, fluid, and he has elite hip flexibility to dip and run in his routes. He’ll convert to YAC situations well, and has the elite blue chip WR mentality.

Cam Coleman, Texas – 6’3, 205, Jr

Coleman doesn’t get enough credit largely because of Smith. He’s headed to his first year with the Longhorns, and is coming off a 708 yard, 56 reception season for Ole Miss. Coleman is an exceptional athlete, likely better than Smith, his route running is much improved, and he has a tremendous amount of lower body agility. Coleman works well in a ton of concepts, is an electric YAC threat with his start stop ability. Coleman has exceptional deceleration, sinks his hips, and drives on the ball or route concept to make a play on the ball. He pairs that with twitch, physical profile, and a mentality to get the ball through contact. He’ll need to improve the drops, but he’s not far off Jeremiah Smith.

Charlie Becker, Indiana – 6’4, 208, R-So

A breakout end to 2025 put Becker on the map, and he’s among the best pure athletes at the position. Becker was arguably the best, or second best receiver on the Hoosiers last year, and he shows an elite ability to explode off the line of scrimmage, runs fluid routes, and he controls his body well. Becker is by far the best contested catch receiver in the class, he’s shown a tremendous ability to go up and get the ball, tracks it well though the air, and his concentration through traffic is exceptional. Becker has smooth feet, quick hips, and he’s an advanced route runner. YAC is a strong point, as is his ability to transition throughout concepts, drive on the ball, and he’s also shown a diverse route tree. Becker has a chance to cement himself as the third receiver in the class with a full season of 2026.

Omarion Miller, Arizona State – 6’2, 210, Jr

The top of the 2027 WR class is big, and Miller’s the smallest but he’s absolutely rocked up and shows a ton of physical traits. A breakout 2025 saw him log 808 yards, 8 touchdowns, and 45 receptions for Colorado, and the Arizona State offense should feature him similar to Jordyn Tyson. Miller is an elite short yardage receiver, has an explosive step off the LOS, works his cuts well, and is juiced up at the position with a ton of athletic traits. miller sinks his hips well, he flys downfield, and as a YAC threat he’s among the best in the class with his lower body athletic ability. His hands are adequate, with a 8% drop rate in 2025, and he’s not one to take away the ball in contested catch situations. I’d also like to see him do better driving back on the ball, and his deceleration looks a bit clunky but he’s effective.

Mario Craver, Texas A&M – 5’9, 175, Jr

The smallest of the top five receivers, he’s a true short yardage receiver. Craver will step into the role that KC Concepcion left, he has a ton of raw traits and his overall immediate acceleration is electric. Craver has an ability to separate from receivers, track the ball over his shoulder, and he has tremendous short area burst out of cuts. Craver does well working from the slot, he can beat press coverage, and he’s a smart receiver showing a ton of IQ against man and zone coverage with an ability to find open spots in coverage, use leverage against DBs, and also stem his route into a concept. I’d like to see him add more muscle to stay dynamic, and I’d also like to see Craver work more on the sideline.

Wyatt Young, Oklahoma State – 6’0, 200, R-So

Young is a prototypical “big slot” he has quick acceleration, is extremely efficient, and a great YAC threat. Young cuts well, has fluid lower body mechanics, and he can work through contact well. He’s able to work a variety of route concepts, has a deep understanding of leverage, coverage schemes, and knows when the flip back to the quarterback. Young had a tendency over the middle to let the ball come to him, and I’d like to see him attack the ball out of his frame more often but the pure raw strength, lower body flexibility, and YAC makes him intriguing. He’ll get the first chance to continue on his 2025 season (70 rec, 1264 yards, 10 TD) at the next level going from North Texas to Oklahoma State.

Eric Singleton, Florida – 5’10, 190, Sr

Nick Marsh, Indiana – 6’3, 205, Jr

Marsh is big, he’s physical, and he’s got good athleticism. He can work at Z, X, and in the slot with good explosiveness, initial acceleration, and he shows a ton of twitch and explosion throughout the route concept start to end. Marsh has good footwork, he’s a sharp route runner, and does best with the ball in his hands using his explosiveness, lower body flexibility, and IQ to his advantage. He’ll need to refine his ability in contested catch situations, with average physicality, and his ability to work back to the ball is an issue as well. Marsh is the 2nd option in the Indiana offense, but he’s excelled the last two seasons with Michigan State focused heavily on the intermediate and short yardage.

Ryan Coleman-Williams, Alabama – 6’0, 185, Jr

I’ll talk quick here, but Williams can easily be at the top of this board, as many thought he’d be after the 2024 season with 860+ yards in his first season. His drop rate spiked in 2025 to 14%, he’s got tremendous athleticism, ball skills, and YAC traits but he struggled to find the ball in space last year, suffered concentration drops, and his route tree was limited. This isn’t a “make or break” year but with a good year Coleman-Williams can easily become a round one name.

Junior Sherrill, Vanderbilt – 5’11, 190, R-Jr

Evan Stewart, Oregon – 6’0, 175, R-So

TJ Moore, Clemson – 6’3, 200, R-So

Ian Strong, California – 6’3, 215, R-Jr

Strong has a tremendous frame, but consistency has flagged him. He’s explosive downfield, has fluid hips, and he does show the ability to be an excellent Z option or 4th receiver. Strong is in a new offense at Cal, which should allow him to be more versatile, and establishing a diverse route tree is big for his draft stock. The frame, lower body athleticism, and strength is crucial and it showed last year in a junior season with 52 receptions, 762 yards, and 5 touchdowns.

Ryan Wingo, Texas – 6’2, 217, R-Jr

Jordan Faison, Notre Dame – 6’0, 210, R-Jr

Faison was a lacrosse player who converted to football, and he’s established a good career with a ton of upside. He was the focal point of the Notre Dame passing game in 2025 with 49 receptions, 640 yards, and 4 touchdowns. Faison has a ways to go technique wise, but his lower body athleticism is exceptional. He has elite start/stop ability, is an exceptional YAC receiver, and understands the game well. Faison shows good hip flexibility, is able to drive on the ball, has soft hands, and can naturally turn to run in space. His deceleration is exceptional, and overall Faison should be a fun gadget player in the NFL.

Cooper Barkate, Miami – 6’1, 200, Sr

Bryant Wescto Jr, Clemson – 6’3, 190, Jr

Nyck Harbor, South Carolina – 6’5, 238, R-Jr

Harbor has the frame and athleticism to be a top draft receiver, with a 6’5, and likely over 240 pound frame with sub 10.2 100M speed. Harbor’s needing to refine his ability as a receiver with a very limited route tree, drop issues, and he’s not shown a ton of tendencies to attack the ball in the air. Harbor is learning the position, but there’s a lot of potential in his future if he can develop the necessary traits.

KJ Duff, Rutgers – 6’6, 225, R-So

Cayden Lee, Ole Miss – 5’11, 190, R-Jr

Mike Matthews, Tennessee – 6’1, 200, R-So

Griffin Wade, Northwestern – 6’2, 200, R-Jr

Wade is fluid, athletic, and there’s a limited ceiling but he has the necessary traits to be a solidified slot receiver. Wade is a sharp route runner, he’s limited in his long speed, and with adequate initial quickness there’s not a ton of immediate long play ability. Wade has good start stop ability, he decelerates well, and is a sharp receiver in his intermediate routes. Additionally, Wade has good YAC skills work across the field, is fluid in space, and he’s able to make defender miss with a good frame. He’ll build on his 880 yard, 8 TD, and 71 reception season in 2026 with his 2nd year at Northwestern (first two at South Dakota State).

Jared Richardson, Duke – 6’2, 216, Sr

Richardson transferred to Duke from Penn, he’s big & physical but lacks some consistent athleticism. He’s been an exceptional receiver at the Ivy League level, attacks the ball, and does have an ability to rise on this list heavily.

Isaiah Satenga, Oklahoma – 5’10, 185, Jr

KD Hutchinson, Western Kentucky – Among the top, if not the best, G5 WR in the class

Josh Derry, UCF – Transfer from FCS, electric speed, true slot guy. Similar to Tre Tucker

title: How Browns' Shedeur Sanders has more than just 2026 QB1 job at stake this season
description: Shedeur Sanders can stop the Browns from taking a QB in the 2027 NFL Draft.
fullText:
How Browns' Shedeur Sanders has more than just 2026 QB1 job at stake this season originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The Cleveland Browns quarterback competition has yet to be fully resolved, as Deshaun Watson and Shedeur Sanders are still battling it out for the QB1 job in 2026.

While this competition rolls along towards the start of the season, there's a significant angle that cannot be forgotten when it comes to Sanders potentially earning the QB1 role.

As The Athletics' Zac Jackson notes, the Browns' second-year QB has much more than the 2026 starting job at stake this year. A strong performance, and Sanders has a real chance to be the franchise QB in Cleveland for years to come.

"All that's at stake is the franchise's immediate future," Jackson writes. "That's a little bit hyperbole, but the Browns are clearly embracing a future-based approach and probably position themselves to select a quarterback early in the 2027 draft. Sanders can change that part of the plan by establishing himself as a starting-quality player and leader this year."

This opportunity for Sanders is easier said than done, as not only does he have to beat out Watson to be named the Browns' starting quarterback, but he also has to keep that job all year.

Any misstep, and the Browns could turn to Watson, effectively ending any chance Sanders has to be the future of the QB position for the Browns.

But if he can have a productive season, thriving with Denzel Boston, KC Concepcion, Isaiah Bond, Harold Fannin Jr., Quinshon Judkins, and Dylan Sampson behind a new-look offensive line under Todd Monken, he could be the starter beyond 2026.

MORE: If Bills trade Josh Allen, this would be the mind-blowing cost

Such a future is going to be very difficult to accomplish for Sanders, as he's going to need to drastically improve upon his 1,400 passing yards, seven touchdowns, and 10 interceptions from this past season in 2026 to get to such a point.

Becoming the franchise quarterback for the Browns is not likely, but it's legitimately possible for Sanders. An incredible 2026 season, along with his cheap rookie contract as a fifth-round pick, could give him a chance to start in 2027.

More likely than not, the Browns wind up drafting someone high in the first-round of the 2027 NFL Draft. But if Sanders breaks out as a star in 2026, he has a real case to be the team's QB1 moving forward.
